title: Incorrect "Selection index out of range" - again
body:
---


Author Name: **Roland Kruse**
Original Redmine Issue: 675 from https://www.veripool.org
Original Date: 2013-09-04
Original Assignee: Wilson Snyder (@wsnyder)

---

This may or may not be related to issue #477. Given the code
```
    reg  [7:0] x;
    wire [3:0] en;
    wire       sel;
    wire       a;
    
    generate
       genvar  g_k;
       for ( g_k = 0; g_k < 8; g_k = g_k + 1 )
         begin: g_index
            always @* begin
               if(g_k<4) begin
                  x[g_k] = (sel == 1'b1) ? 1'b1 : (en[g_k] == 1'b0) ? 1'b1 : a;
               end else begin
                  x[g_k] = (sel == 1'b0) ? 1'b1 : (en[g_k-4] == 1'b0) ? 1'b1 : a;
               end
            end
         end
    endgenerate

```
I get the errors
```
  %Error: t/t_sel_index.v:36: Selection index out of range: 4:4 outside 3:0
  %Error: t/t_sel_index.v:36: Selection index out of range: 5:5 outside 3:0
  %Error: t/t_sel_index.v:36: Selection index out of range: 6:6 outside 3:0
  %Error: t/t_sel_index.v:36: Selection index out of range: 7:7 outside 3:0

```
The test case files are attached.
It seems like en[g_k] is evaluated even when g_k is greater than 3, despite the if (g_k<4) condition. Interestingly, that seems to not be the case with en[g_k-4] when g_k is less than 4.

Indeed, if I remove the condition and just have
```
    generate
       genvar  g_k;
       for ( g_k = 0; g_k < 8; g_k = g_k + 1 )
         begin: g_index

            always @* begin
               x[g_k] = (sel == 1'b1) ? 1'b1 : (en[g_k-4] == 1'b0) ? 1'b1 : a;
            end
         end
    endgenerate

```
it compiles without error even though the index definitiely is out of range now when g_k is less than 4...
